channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Masses of mourners attend the funeral of Hezbollah leader Nasrallah, 5 months after his killing
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of people packed into a stadium in Beirut and nearby streets on Sunday for the funeral of Hezbollah's former leader, Hassan Nasrallah, nearly five months after he was killed in an Israeli airstrike.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, ABBY SEWELL and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: EU leaders will hold an emergency summit on Ukraine and European security
item.description: The leaders of the 27 European Union countries will hold an emergency summit on March 6 about next steps for Ukraine and Europe’s security.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Sudan’s military continues its field advances, lifting RSF siege of a crucial city
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Sudan’s military on Sunday broke a more than year-long siege on the crucial city of Obeid, restoring access to a strategic area in the south-central region and strengthening crucial supply routes in its nearly two years of war against a notorious paramilitary group, officials said.
item.dc:creator: BY SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pope Francis in critical condition with early kidney failure but remains alert as prayers pour in
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Sunday and blood tests show early kidney failure but he remains alert and “well-oriented,” and attended Mass, the Vatican said, as the 88-year-old pontiff battles pneumonia and a complex lung infection.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bessent says US-Ukraine deal has 'implicit' economic security guarantee
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said a U.S.-Ukraine deal featuring “strategic minerals, energy and state-owned enterprises” has an “implicit” economic security guarantee.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says he'd be ready to give up presidency if it brought peace, NATO membership
item.description: “If to achieve peace, you really need me to give up my post, I’m ready,” Zelenskyy said in response to a journalist's question on whether he’d trade his office for peace. “I can trade it for NATO.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy says he may be forced to sign mineral agreement with U.S. in exchange for continued aid
item.description: Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says he may be forced to sign an economic agreement with the United States that would ensure continued aid for Ukraine in return for the U.S. making a profit from minerals in the country.
item.dc:creator: Justin Spike, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel delays release of over 600 Palestinian prisoners after hostages freed
item.description: Israel delayed the release of more than 600 Palestinian prisoners on Saturday after six hostages were freed that afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: French aircraft carrier stages combat drills with Filipinos in disputed sea and visits Philippines
item.description: ABOARD THE CHARLES DE GAULLE, Philippines (AP) — France’s nuclear-powered aircraft carrier and accompanying warships were in the Philippines on Sunday after holding combat drills with Filipino forces in the disputed South China Sea in a show of firepower that would likely antagonize China.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ AND JOEAL CALUPITAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Afghan women’s radio station will resume broadcasts after Taliban lift suspension
item.description: An Afghan women’s radio station will resume broadcasts after the Taliban lifted their suspension over alleged cooperation with an overseas TV channel. Radio Begum launched on International Women’s Day in March 2021, five months before the Taliban seized power amid the chaotic withdrawal of U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Wounded, recovered and back to war. Ukrainian soldiers are returning to battle after amputation
item.description: DONETSK REGION, Ukraine (AP) — The Ukrainian intelligence soldier doesn’t know how long his clinical death lasted after an explosive detonated beneath him. All Andrii Rubliuk remembers is overwhelming cold, darkness and fear.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, EVGENIY MALOLETKA and VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel delays release of Palestinian prisoners, citing 'humiliating' handovers of hostages
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel says the release of hundreds of Palestinian prisoners is delayed “until the release of the next hostages has been assured, and without the humiliating ceremonies” at handovers of Israeli captives in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, MOHAMMED JAHJOUH and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope now in ‘critical condition’ after pneumonia, Vatican warns
item.description: Pope Francis was placed in “critical condition” on Saturday, the Vatican said, just four days after the religious leader contracted pneumonia. “The condition of the Holy Father continues to be critical. Therefore, as explained yesterday, the Pope is not out of danger.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Pope Francis is in critical condition after a long respiratory crisis, requiring oxygen at high flow
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis was in critical condition Saturday after he suffered a prolonged asthmatic respiratory crisis while being treated for pneumonia and a complex lung infection, the Vatican said. The 88-year-old pope, who remains conscious, received “high flows” of oxygen to help him breathe.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and SILVIA STELLACCI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump-Putin summit preparations are underway, Russia says
item.description: Preparations are underway for a face-to-face meeting between U.S. President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin, Russia's deputy foreign minister said Saturday. It marked a clear departure from Western efforts to isolate Moscow over its war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis in critical condition, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis suffered an asthmatic respiratory crisis that required oxygen, the Associated Press reported. The 88-year-old pope has been hospitalized for over a week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vatican says Pope Francis is in critical condition
item.description: The 88-year-old Francis, who has been hospitalized for a week with a complex lung infection, also received blood transfusions after tests showed a condition associated with anemia, the Vatican said in a late update.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Houthis target US fighter jet, drone with SAM missiles for first time
item.description: Houthi rebels fired air missile at United States military fighter jets. While the missile did not strike the jet, the attempted attack escalated the altercation between the two sides.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hungarians will decide whether Ukraine can join the European Union, Orbán says
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Hungary will be the one to decide whether Ukraine is able to achieve its hopes of joining the European Union in the future, Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán said Saturday in an escalation of his adversarial posture toward Hungary's war-ravaged neighbor.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas releases 6 more hostages, signals readiness for next phase of shaky ceasefire
item.description: Hamas on Saturday released six Israeli hostages in exchange for hundreds of Palestinian prisoners, signaling readiness for the next phase of a shaky ceasefire. Avera Mengistu and Tal Shoham were handed to the Red Cross and eventually to Israel in the southern Gazan city of Rafah.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Netanyahu decries release of wrong body as a ceasefire violation. Hamas pledges to investigate
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu vowed revenge Friday for what he described as a “cruel and malicious violation” of the ceasefire agreement after authorities determined that a body released by Hamas was not an Israeli mother of two small boys, as the militant group had prom
item.dc:creator: AREEJ HAZBOUN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Suspect arrested after stabbing at Berlin's Holocaust memorial near US embassy
item.description: German police arrested a suspect in a stabbing attack at Berlin's Holocaust memorial on Friday. No motive has been given for the attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Remains of Shiri Bibas, mom of two killed, allegedly returned to Israel following Hamas' broken promise
item.description: The remains of a woman killed while in captivity by Hamas was supposedly handed over to Israel on Friday, a day after her body was supposed to be brought back.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas’s failure to return Israeli mother’s body strains ceasefire
item.description: A tenuous ceasefire between Israel and Hamas has been strained by outrage over Hamas’s alleged transfer of an unknown body to Israel in the place of Shiri Bibas, the Israeli mother who was kidnapped along with her two young children during Hamas’s Oct. 7, 2023, attack on Israel.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump: Zelensky 'has no cards,' 'shouldn't be at meetings' with Russia
item.description: President Trump lashed out at Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Friday, saying he did not think it was important for Zelensky to attend negotiations about the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu calls for 'revenge' after Hamas returns wrong remains
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is vowing revenge after Hamas delivered the body of an anonymous woman instead of Shiri Bibas in violation of the current ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Singer of Canadian anthem at 4 Nations Face-Off changes lyric to protest Trump's 51st state remarks
item.description: The anthem singer who performed the Canadian anthem prior to the 4 Nations Face-Off championship game Thursday night changed a lyric in “O Canada” as a response to U.S. President Donald Trump's repeated remarks about making the country the 51st state.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO and KEN POWTAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: China issued 'disconcerting' warning of live-fire exercises to planes flying above, Australia says
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Airliners were over the Tasman Sea crossing between Australia and New Zealand when the Chinese navy warned they were flying over a secret live-fire exercise, Australian Defense Minister Richard Marles said on Friday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK and CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-MCLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas claims remains of mother of two young boys mixed with other human remains after Israeli airstrike
item.description: Shiri Bibas, who was murdered by Hamas terrorists, while in captivity was not among the bodies returned to Israel and Hamas officials say it's the result of an Israeli air strike.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Remains of young mom Shiri Bibas, taken hostage and killed by Hamas, not returned despite promise, Israel says
item.description: The mother of a woman abducted with her two children and husband was not among the slain hostages returned to Israel by Hamas on Thursday, Israel said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's envoy arrives in Kyiv as US policy shifts toward Russia
item.description: Keith Kellogg, the U.S. special envoy for Ukraine and Russia, arrived in Kyiv on Wednesday for talks with President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and military commanders, as the U.S. shifted its policy away from years of efforts to isolate Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: DeepSeek accused of sharing user data with TikTok owner ByteDance
item.description: "We confirmed DeepSeek communicating with ByteDance," a South Korean data protection regulator told Yonhap News Agency.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Hundreds of FAA workers laid off amid staffing shortages, raising safety concerns
item.description: Hundreds of probationary workers with the Federal Aviation Administration were laid off over the weekend, raising concerns about air traffic safety as the agency struggles to meet hiring goals amid increased air traffic.
item.dc:creator: Ben Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: US leaders in Saudi Arabia for peace talks on Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said he will not accept a deal without a seat at the negotiating table.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation
